mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity cash flows, directly reducing present value of future earnings and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand across durable and non-durable goods, forcing firms to cut production and guidance, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with forward return mean > 0.0% and Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals rapid capacity utilization and supply-side tightening, which compresses input costs and boosts near-term corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following INDPRO increases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power during early-cycle phases (Rank IC < 0.00).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 190 while the federal funds rate remains below 3.5%, real rates turn deeply negative, triggering expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that immediately penalizes equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL crosses above 190 while FEDFUNDS remains below 3.5%
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered periods, or the inflation-rate gap loses predictive significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer purchasing power, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods following PAYEMS increases, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP signals an economic recession that forces widespread earnings downgrades and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 crosses below prior quarter level
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of quarters following GDP contractions, or the signal fails to separate recessionary from expansionary periods (AUC < 0.55).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates robust output growth alongside labor market slack, which reduces wage inflation pressures and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with mean forward return < 0.0% and Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.
